req.title: Recurring transfer
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines two equivalence partitions by tier (standard/premium), a hard per-occurrence cap per tier, an explicit error code (422) for limit and frequency violations, and an expected persistence + next-run calculation. Tests are designed using EP for valid-tier happy paths, BVA for the exact cap values, NEG for over-limit and unsupported-frequency rejection, DT for the cross-product of tier x amount, and STC for the schedule-creation state transition. Fixtures and helpers are referenced as placeholders to keep each test self-contained and under 40 lines.

tests.name: test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit
preconditions: API is reachable and auth token is valid
actions: Authenticate as a premium-tier customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=5000, frequency=monthly
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains next_run date calculated from today + one month
trace: R-FIN-005: premium tier may schedule up to $10,000 per occurrence
R-FIN-005: expected outcome - schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
def test_premium_recurring_transfer_within_limit(premium_auth_token, api_client, assert_next_run):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "source_account_id": "<fixture:premium_source_account>",
        "destination_account_id": "<fixture:destination_account>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body, "Missing schedule_id in response"
    assert "next_run" in body, "Missing next_run in response"
    assert_next_run(body["next_run"], frequency="monthly")
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/transfers/recurring/{body['schedule_id']}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["schedule_id"] == body["schedule_id"]

tests.name: test_standard_transfer_above_cap_rejected
preconditions: API is reachable and auth token is valid
actions: Authenticate as a standard-tier customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=2501 (exceeds $2,500 cap), frequency=weekly
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating per-occurrence limit exceeded
trace: R-FIN-005: non-premium accounts capped at $2,500 per occurrence
R-FIN-005: error pathway - 422 when per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_standard_transfer_above_cap_rejected(standard_auth_token, api_client, assert_no_schedule):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2501,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "weekly",
        "source_account_id": "<fixture:standard_source_account>",
        "destination_account_id": "<fixture:destination_account>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "No error detail in 422 response"
    error_text = str(body.get("error") or body.get("detail", "")).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_text or "exceed" in error_text, f"Unexpected error message: {error_text}"
    assert_no_schedule(standard_auth_token, amount=2501)

tests.name: test_standard_transfer_at_exact_cap_accepted
preconditions: API is reachable and auth token is valid
actions: Authenticate as a standard-tier customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=2500 (exactly at cap), frequency=weekly
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains next_run calculated from today + one week
trace: R-FIN-005: non-premium accounts capped at $2,500 - boundary inclusive
R-FIN-005: expected outcome - schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
def test_standard_transfer_at_exact_cap_accepted(standard_auth_token, api_client, assert_next_run):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "weekly",
        "source_account_id": "<fixture:standard_source_account>",
        "destination_account_id": "<fixture:destination_account>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body, "Missing schedule_id in response"
    assert "next_run" in body, "Missing next_run in response"
    assert_next_run(body["next_run"], frequency="weekly")
    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/transfers/recurring/{body['schedule_id']}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["amount"] == 2500

tests.name: test_premium_transfer_above_cap_rejected
preconditions: API is reachable and auth token is valid
actions: Authenticate as a premium-tier customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=10001 (one cent above premium cap), frequency=monthly
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response error indicates per-occurrence limit exceeded
trace: R-FIN-005: premium tier capped at $10,000 - amount 10001 is above limit
R-FIN-005: error pathway - 422 when per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_premium_transfer_above_cap_rejected(premium_auth_token, api_client, assert_no_schedule):
    payload = {
        "amount": 10001,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "source_account_id": "<fixture:premium_source_account>",
        "destination_account_id": "<fixture:destination_account>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error") or body.get("detail", "")).lower()
    assert "limit" in error_text or "exceed" in error_text
    assert_no_schedule(premium_auth_token, amount=10001)

tests.name: test_unsupported_frequency_rejected
preconditions: API is reachable and auth token is valid
actions: Authenticate as a premium-tier customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=1000 and frequency=hourly (unsupported value)
Capture HTTP status and response body
trace: R-FIN-005: error pathway - 422 when frequency unsupported
tests.executable:
def test_unsupported_frequency_rejected(premium_auth_token, api_client, assert_no_schedule):
    payload = {
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "hourly",
        "source_account_id": "<fixture:premium_source_account>",
        "destination_account_id": "<fixture:destination_account>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error") or body.get("detail", "")).lower()
    assert "frequency" in error_text or "unsupported" in error_text, f"Unexpected: {error_text}"
    assert_no_schedule(premium_auth_token, amount=1000)

tests.name: test_tier_x_amount_decision_table
preconditions: Both standard and premium customer accounts exist
API is reachable and auth tokens are valid
actions: For each (tier, amount, expected_status) row in decision table, POST /transfers/recurring
Capture status code per row and compare to expectation
trace: R-FIN-005: decision table cross-product of tier and amount boundaries
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("token_fixture,amount,expected_status", [
    ("standard_auth_token", 1000,  201),
    ("standard_auth_token", 2500,  201),
    ("standard_auth_token", 2501,  422),
    ("premium_auth_token",  2501,  201),
    ("premium_auth_token",  10000, 201),
    ("premium_auth_token",  10001, 422),
])
def test_tier_x_amount_decision_table(token_fixture, amount, expected_status, request, api_client):
    token = request.getfixturevalue(token_fixture)
    src = "<fixture:standard_source_account>" if "standard" in token_fixture else "<fixture:premium_source_account>"
    payload = {
        "amount": amount,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "monthly",
        "source_account_id": src,
        "destination_account_id": "<fixture:destination_account>"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, (
        f"tier={token_fixture} amount={amount}: expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_schedule_creation_state_transition
preconditions: No pre-existing recurring schedule for this customer/account pair
API is reachable and auth token is valid
actions: Verify no schedule exists (GET returns 404 or empty list)
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=1000, frequency=weekly to create schedule
Verify schedule transitions to active state (GET returns status=active)
DELETE /transfers/recurring/{schedule_id} to cancel
Verify schedule transitions to cancelled state
trace: R-FIN-005: state transition none -> active -> cancelled
R-FIN-005: schedule persisted with next run calculated
tests.executable:
def test_schedule_creation_state_transition(standard_auth_token, api_client, unique_account_pair):
    src, dst = unique_account_pair
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    list_resp = api_client.get("/transfers/recurring", headers=headers, params={"source_account_id": src})
    assert list_resp.status_code == 200
    assert all(s["source_account_id"] != src for s in list_resp.json().get("schedules", []))
    payload = {"amount": 1000, "currency": "USD", "frequency": "weekly",
               "source_account_id": src, "destination_account_id": dst}
    create_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/recurring", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    schedule_id = create_resp.json()["schedule_id"]
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/transfers/recurring/{schedule_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "active"
    assert "next_run" in get_resp.json()
    del_resp = api_client.delete(f"/transfers/recurring/{schedule_id}", headers=headers)
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204)
    final_resp = api_client.get(f"/transfers/recurring/{schedule_id}", headers=headers)
    assert final_resp.json()["status"] == "cancelled"
